seo.title: Building Multi-agent orchestration patterns with LangChai...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Multi-agent orchestration patterns with LangChain and LangGraph.

content.intro: This guide provides a structured approach to implementing AI agents with tool integration, focusing on reliability, observability, and cost control. Follow these steps to build and debug agent workflows using common frameworks.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of function calling patterns

steps.title: Define tool function schemas
steps.explanation: Create explicit JSON schemas for all tools your agent will use. Use the 'tool' parameter in LangChain's StructuredOutputParser to enforce strict formatting.

code.content:
from langchain.output_parsers import StructuredOutputParser
from langchain_core.pydantic_v1 import BaseModel, Field

class WeatherTool(BaseModel):
    location: str = Field(description="City name")
    unit: str = Field(description="Temperature unit", enum=["C", "F"])

parser = StructuredOutputParser.from_instructions("Return a JSON object with location and unit fields.")

pitfalls: Using vague schema definitions leading to hallucinated tool calls
Forgetting to include required fields in function parameters

steps.title: Implement agent state management
steps.explanation: Create a state class that tracks tool calls, responses, and execution context. Use LangGraph's StateGraph to define workflow transitions.

code.content:
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END
from typing import TypedDict

class AgentState(TypedDict):
    messages: list
    tool_calls: list
    context: dict

workflow = StateGraph(AgentState)
workflow.add_node("tool_call", tool_call_node)
workflow.add_edge(START, "tool_call")
workflow.add_edge("tool_call", END)

pitfalls: Not persisting state between steps causing context loss
Incorrect edge definitions leading to workflow deadlocks

steps.title: Add observability hooks
steps.explanation: Integrate LangSmith to track agent execution. Use the 'langsmith' callback to log tool calls and responses for debugging.

code.content:
from langchain_community.callbacks import LangSmithTracer

tracer = LangSmithTracer()
tracer.set_project("agent-debugging")

# Add to LLM call:
llm = OpenAI(callbacks=[tracer])

pitfalls: Forgetting to set the project name leading to unorganized traces
Not capturing all tool call outputs in logs

steps.title: Implement cost monitoring
steps.explanation: Track token usage and API costs using middleware. Add a counter that triggers alerts when thresholds are exceeded.
pitfalls: Not accounting for different pricing models across LLM providers
Ignoring background cost monitoring during long-running tasks

steps.title: Add human-in-the-loop validation
steps.explanation: Implement a step that requires human approval for critical decisions. Use a tool that returns a 'needs_approval' flag.
pitfalls: Not handling approval timeouts leading to workflow stalls

content.conclusion: By following these steps, you've implemented a robust agent workflow with proper tool integration, observability, and safety controls. Regularly review logs in LangSmith and adjust cost thresholds based on usage patterns.
